title: MAS Spawn Manifest
description: Strict deterministic contract between Tier 1 (LLM Task Decomposer) and Tier 2 (Mechanical TS Engine). The decomposer produces this manifest; the TS Engine validates it against this schema, then executes it verbatim. The engine is a dumb pipe — it never reinterprets, re-routes, or re-decomposes. All routing, phase chains, skill injections, and context budgets are declared explicitly here by the decomposer. Mechanical gates (edge-judge, ast-aggregator, global-judge) are pure TypeScript and consume the fields declared in this manifest as their operational parameters.
$comment: AI agents: Read schemas/manifest-field-guide.md (AI-readable reference) and schemas/manifest-examples.json (copy-adapt templates) instead of this raw schema. This JSON Schema is for machine validation only.

manifest_version.description: Semantic version of the manifest schema. Must match the engine's supported version.

workflow_id.description: Unique identifier for this workflow run. Generated by the decomposer.

source.description: Provenance: which LLM agent and model produced this manifest. This is the single non-deterministic seam — the engine validates everything downstream of it.

decomposer.description: Name of the Tier-1 decomposer agent that produced this manifest.

decomposer_model.description: Model ID used by the decomposer, for audit trail.

user_message.description: Raw user intent string. Used for !quick intent signal detection and HITL context. The engine does not interpret this — it passes it through to HITL.

domain_set.description: Distinct domains touched by this workflow. Drives global skill injection set.

routing_decision.description: Routing mode determined by the decomposer via complexity scoring C(T). The engine reads this to select execution mode but does not recompute it. fast_lane = single-node, trimmed phase_chain; full_dag = multi-node DAG with staged parallelism.

complexity.description: Complexity score C(T) and its components, for audit. The decomposer computes this; the engine logs it but does not recompute it.

score.description: Composite complexity score C(T) = (H_norm + D_JS + I_norm) / k, where k = count of active components. Range [0, 1].

h_norm.description: Normalized Shannon entropy of file-change distribution.

d_js.description: Jensen-Shannon divergence across domains. Present only when >= 2 domains.

i_norm.description: Max mutual information across task pairs. Present only when > 1 task.

user_intent_signal.description: True if user_message starts with !quick (overrides all conditions, sets C(T) = 0).

requirements.description: Stable requirement IDs (R-IDs) that the mechanical global-judge uses as its coverage-map keys. Every requirement the user asked for must appear here. The global-judge computes covered/total by mapping each R-ID to nodes that declare satisfies: [R-ID] and verifying their patches touch acceptance_files.

nodes.description: DAG nodes. Each node is one atomic task (or one synthetic pre-node). Pre-nodes (e.g., DB migrations) participate in edges and levels identically to task nodes — the engine treats them uniformly.

edges.description: Dependency edges of the DAG. The engine validates that levels is a valid topological sort of these edges. Edge reasons are classified by the decomposer per mas-routing rules.

levels.description: Topological levels — each inner array is a set of node IDs with zero unresolved incoming edges from prior levels. The engine dispatches all nodes in one level via Promise.all() (true parallelism). Must be a valid topological sort of edges.

gates.description: Fan-in mechanical gates scheduled by the engine. edge-judge is per-node (declared in each node's phase_chain); ast-aggregator and global-judge are workflow-level (declared here).

per_level.description: Gates that run after each topological level completes (fan-in across all lanes in that level).

ast_aggregator.description: If true, the TS ast-aggregator runs after each level, merging all Edge-Judge-APPROVED patches in that level into a consolidated patch with collision detection.

terminal.description: Gates that run once after all levels complete (terminal fan-in).

global_judge.description: If true, the TS global-judge runs on the final consolidated patch, computing requirements coverage map (R-ID ↔ node.satisfies), detecting MISSING_REQUIREMENT, UNPLANNED_CHANGE, CORRUPTED_MUTATION, and REGRESSION_VECTOR.

hitl.description: Human-in-the-loop boundary. The engine assembles a proposed HITL payload from gate outputs and returns it to the Tier-0 entry mode for presentation. The engine does NOT present HITL itself — that is the LLM periphery's job.

required.description: Whether HITL confirmation is required before applying the consolidated patch to the workspace.

max_feedback_loops.description: Maximum feedback re-entry loops before the engine pauses and escalates to the user.

reentry_routing.description: Feedback category → re-entry point mapping. The engine uses this to route HITL feedback deterministically. Keys are feedback categories; values describe which phase to re-enter at.

metadata.description: Workflow-level summary metadata. The engine validates these against actual counts.

max_width.description: Maximum number of nodes in any single level (parallelism width).

domain.description: Technology domain. Drives which skill directories the engine injects.

concern.description: Architectural concern category. Drives concern-specific skill injection. null = no specific concern detected.

language.description: Programming language of the target files. The mechanical edge-judge uses this to select its compile/lint tool.

context_tier.description: Context tier the engine's dehydrator prepares for this phase. tier1 = signatures only (<=1K tokens); tier2 = + type interfaces + import graph (<=2K); tier3 = full file content (<=4K sandbox); diff = unified diff of changed lines only.

requirement.description: A single user requirement with a stable ID. The global-judge coverage map keys on these IDs.

examples.statement: Add structured logging to UserService.create method

id.description: Stable requirement identifier. Referenced by node.satisfies[].

statement.description: Human-readable statement of what the requirement demands.

acceptance_files.description: Files that must be touched (modified or created) for this requirement to be considered COVERED by the global-judge.

acceptance_symbols.description: Specific symbols (functions/types/classes) that must be present or modified. Optional refinement of acceptance_files.

scope_lines.description: Line range scope constraint for a single file. The mechanical edge-judge checks SCOPE_ESCAPE: any diff line outside these ranges in this file triggers LANE_REJECTED.

delta.description: Declared import or export delta. The decomposer predicts these; the ast-aggregator uses them for inter-node collision detection (IMPORT_CONFLICT, SIGNATURE_DIVERGENCE, ORDERING_VIOLATION), and the global-judge compares them against actual patch deltas to detect UNPLANNED_CHANGE.

phase.description: A single phase in a node's explicit phase_chain. The engine executes phases in array order. type=llm phases spawn a generic LLM agent with injected skills; type=mechanical phases run a TS gate.
Phase name. discover/architect/implement are LLM-only; verify/fix are LLM-only; gate is mechanical-only (edge-judge).

agent.description: Generic LLM agent to spawn. Required when type=llm. Must be one of the 5 generic compute agents.

gate.description: Mechanical gate to run. Required when phase=gate. Only edge-judge is per-node; ast-aggregator and global-judge are workflow-level (declared in top-level gates).

skills.description: Skill names to inject into the agent at runtime. The engine reads skills/<name>/SKILL.md for each and embeds the content in the agent's context. Required when type=llm. Agents must NOT self-load skills — injection is engine-driven for determinism.

items.description: Skill directory name under ~/.config/opencode/skills/. Must match ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$.

context_tier.description: Context tier the engine dehydrates for this phase. Required when type=llm.

conditional.description: Whether this phase always runs or only on a condition. always = run unconditionally; on_verify_fail = run only if the preceding verify phase returned FAIL. The engine evaluates the condition mechanically.

max_steps.description: Maximum agentic iterations for the LLM agent in this phase. Optional. If unset, the engine uses its default.

retry_budget.description: Re-spin budget for this node's lane. The engine retries the fix→gate cycle up to max_respins times on edge-judge REJECTED. On exceeding, the lane is marked UNRESOLVABLE_ANOMALY and escalated.

max_respins.description: Maximum fix→gate re-spin attempts. 0 = no retry (fast_lane default). 2 = full_dag default.

context_budget.description: Token budget for this node's context across all phases. Enforced by the engine's dehydrator.

max_tokens.description: Maximum total tokens for dehydrated context per phase. Default 4000 per mas-integrity sandbox.

node.description: A single DAG node — one atomic task or one synthetic pre-node.

id.description: Unique node identifier. Referenced by edges and levels.

node_type.description: task = a real code mutation task; pre_node = a synthetic dependency (e.g., DB migration) that must complete before dependent task nodes.

satisfies.description: Requirement IDs (R-IDs) that this node's mutation fulfills. The global-judge uses this to compute coverage: for each R-ID, at least one node declaring satisfies:[R-ID] must have an APPROVED patch touching acceptance_files. Required for task nodes; must be empty for pre_nodes.

target_files.description: Files this node is authorized to modify. The edge-judge checks SCOPE_ESCAPE against this list — any diff touching a file not in this list triggers LANE_REJECTED.

scope_lines.description: Per-file line range constraints. If present, the edge-judge rejects any diff line outside these ranges in the corresponding file. If absent for a file, the entire file is in scope.

mutation.description: Single implementable instruction describing what the implementer should do. One concern, one mutation.

output_files.description: Files expected to be produced or modified by this node. Subset of target_files plus any new files. Used by ast-aggregator for dependency matrix.

imports_delta.description: Declared import changes. The ast-aggregator uses these for IMPORT_CONFLICT detection between nodes and the global-judge compares them against actual patch imports.

exports_delta.description: Declared export changes. The ast-aggregator uses these for SIGNATURE_DIVERGENCE detection and the global-judge compares them against actual patch exports.

touches_symbols.description: Symbols (function/type/class names) this node's mutation touches. The global-judge uses these for REGRESSION_VECTOR scan: any untouched file depending on a touched symbol is flagged.

phase_chain.description: Explicit ordered list of phases the engine executes for this node. The engine runs them verbatim in array order — no derivation, no skipping (unless conditional=on_verify_fail evaluates false). This is the locked explicit-phase-chain decision: maximal provability.

edge.description: A dependency edge in the DAG. from must complete before to starts. The engine validates that levels is a valid topological sort respecting all edges.

reason.description: Edge classification reason per mas-routing rules. The engine logs this for audit but does not reclassify.

reentry_point.description: Where to re-enter the pipeline when the user provides feedback of a given category. The engine uses this to route deterministically after HITL.

reenter_phase.description: Which phase to re-enter at for affected nodes.

target_node_strategy.description: Which nodes to re-run. affected = only nodes touched by the feedback; all = re-run entire workflow; redecompose = discard manifest, return to decomposer.
